methods.description: Preparation of sediment samples and cleaning of foraminifera for Nd isotope composition analysis.

steps.description: All samples were freeze-dried and weighed and an aliquot of 2.5 g of each sample was separated and ground using an agate pestle and mortar.
steps.searchable_summary: freeze-dry, weigh, and grind sediment samples

steps.description: The remaining sample aliquot was wet sieved, oven dried and separated into size fractions ready for microscope work.
steps.searchable_summary: wet sieve, oven dry, and size fraction sediment samples

steps.description: Approximately 35 mg of mixed planktonic foraminifera were picked from the >350-μm size fraction for extraction of Nd isotope composition.
steps.searchable_summary: pick foraminifera from size fraction for Nd isotope extraction

steps.description: The shells were crushed between two glass plates to open the chambers, and were then ultrasonicated with alternate rinses of distilled (MQ) water and ethanol to remove any detrital particles.
steps.searchable_summary: crush and ultrasonicate foraminifera shells to clean

steps.description: A final visual check for removal of particles was performed under the microscope before dissolution of the foraminifera samples in 0.5 M HNO3.
steps.searchable_summary: perform visual check and dissolve foraminifera in HNO3

methods.description: Extraction and leaching of detrital fraction from sediment samples for further analysis.

steps.description: To extract the detrital fraction, the 2.5 g powdered aliquots were leached following the procedures fully described in refs. 52,53.
steps.searchable_summary: leach powdered aliquots to extract detrital fraction

steps.description: Samples were rinsed with distilled (MQ) water, followed by a decarbonation procedure using an acetic acid/sodium acetate buffer (with a pH around 4).
steps.searchable_summary: rinse and decarbonate samples with acetic acid buffer

steps.description: A hydroxylamine hydrochloride solution was used as a leaching agent for removing the Fe-Mn oxyhydroxide fraction, buffered with acetic acid/sodium hydroxide to reach pH 4.
steps.searchable_summary: use hydroxylamine hydrochloride to leach Fe-Mn oxyhydroxide

steps.description: This initial leach solution was removed and an additional 12 h of leaching with newly added leaching solution was carried out to completely remove any authigenic coatings.
steps.searchable_summary: perform additional leaching to remove authigenic coatings

steps.description: After a triple rinse with MQ water and oven drying, an aliquot of 0.05 g of the remaining detrital fraction underwent total dissolution via hotplate digestion using a mixture of HNO3, HF and HCl.
steps.searchable_summary: rinse, dry, and dissolve detrital fraction using hotplate digestion

methods.description: Separation and purification of Nd and Sr isotopes from sample solutions using ion chromatography.

steps.description: Further separation of Nd and Sr and purification of the sample solutions was carried out ion chromatographically.
steps.searchable_summary: perform ion chromatographic separation of Nd and Sr

steps.description: All samples were put through cation exchange columns (AG50W-X12 resin, mesh 200-400 µm) to collect rare earth elements (REEs) and, in the case of the detrital samples, the Sr cut also was collected.
steps.searchable_summary: use cation exchange columns to collect REEs and Sr

steps.description: Nd was separated from the other REEs (Ln Spec resin columns, mesh 50-100 µm) and Sr was separated from Rb and other interfering elements (Sr spec resin column, mesh 50-100 µm).
steps.searchable_summary: separate Nd and Sr using specific resin columns

methods.description: Measurement and correction of Nd and Sr isotope ratios using mass spectrometry.

steps.description: Isotope ratios were measured on a Nu Instruments Multiple Collector Inductively Coupled Plasma Mass Spectrometer (MC-ICPMS) at GEOMAR.
The 143Nd/144Nd results were mass bias corrected to a 146Nd/144Nd ratio of 0.7219 and normalized to the accepted JNdi-1 standard 143Nd/144Nd value of 0.512115.
steps.searchable_summary: correct and normalize Nd isotope ratios

steps.description: For downcore samples, εNd(t) values were calculated by correcting the measured εNd(0) for ingrowth of 143Nd using an Sm/Nd ratio of 0.139 for uncleaned foraminiferal calcite and 0.109 for detrital material.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate εNd(t) values for downcore samples

steps.description: After correction for Kr and Rb interferences, Sr results were mass bias corrected to an 86Sr/88Sr ratio of 0.1194 and normalized to the widely accepted 87Sr/86Sr value of 0.710245 for the NIST SRM987 standard.
steps.searchable_summary: correct and normalize Sr isotope ratios
